objective: Complete a full end-to-end PM case from problem framing to leadership update, self-score across all 12 Reforge competencies with evidence, and write a personal next-30-days improvement plan.

realWorldAnchor.context: SaaSCraft is a fictional Indian B2B project management SaaS serving SMEs — similar to companies like Zoho, Freshworks, or Backlog in their early growth phase. It is fictional so you can use all the data given without worrying about accuracy, and so the assessment tests synthesis of all 21 days of learning rather than India company knowledge specifically.

parallelSkillThread: OKRs (Day 21 — closes the thread from Day 15): Evaluate your own Day 1-21 learning as OKRs — did you hit them? Revise them if needed.

tasks.title: The complete PM — synthesising execution, strategy, and people skills into one role
tasks.summary: This reading is a return to Day 1 — the same Ravi Mehta competency model that opened the programme. Reading it again on Day 21 is intentional: the goal is to see how much more concrete and specific each of the 12 competencies now feels after 21 days of application. What was abstract on Day 1 (what does 'Fluency with Data' actually mean?) should now map directly to your RCA trees, SQL readings, cohort tables, and metric investigation frameworks.
keyTakeaways: The 12 competencies are not equally weighted at the APM level — Product Execution (Feature Specification, Product Delivery, Product Quality) and Customer Insight (Fluency with Data, Voice of Customer) are the first two pillars hiring managers evaluate for entry-level PMs

Product Strategy competencies (Business Outcome Ownership, Product Vision, Strategic Impact) become the differentiator for promotion from APM to PM — you are not expected to outperform at these on Day 1, but you are expected to show awareness

Influencing People competencies (Stakeholder Management, Team Leadership, Managing Up) are the hardest to demonstrate in interviews and the most important in the actual job — they are what Day 20 was designed to surface
Self-assessment on competencies is a PM discipline, not just a career exercise — the best PMs run a personal retrospective every quarter and adjust their learning plan accordingly
The gap between 'Needs Focus' and 'On Track' on any competency is usually one thing: deliberate practice with real feedback, not more reading

tasks.promptTitle: OKR self-evaluation: did you hit your Day 15 OKRs? What are your next-quarter OKRs?
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are a PM coach and OKR facilitator.

Role: Help me close the OKR thread from Day 15 by evaluating my learning OKRs against what I actually did in 21 days, and writing a revised set for the next phase.

On Day 15, I wrote OKRs for my 21-day learning programme. Today is Day 21 — the last day. I need to evaluate whether I hit them, and use that evaluation to write my next-quarter OKRs.

Context of what I covered across 21 days:
- Week 1 (Days 1-7): PM role, MECE, JTBD/Personas, Problem Framing, RICE, CIRCLES, Week 1 integration
- Week 2 (Days 8-14): North Star + AARRR + SQL, Metric drops + RCA, A/B testing, Analytics + Cohort retention, PRDs + User stories + Figma, Roadmapping + Stakeholder comms, Week 2 integration
- Week 3 (Days 15-21): Strategy + Market sizing + OKRs, Indian product landscape, Bharat vs India + ONDC, Case practice (product design + estimation + API basics), Case practice (metrics + strategy), Behavioral cases + Influencing people, Final integration

Activity steps:

Step 1 — Share your Day 15 OKRs
Paste the OKRs you wrote on Day 15 here. [Paste your work them]

Step 2 — Honest evaluation
For each Key Result, give yourself a score: 0% (not started), 25% (early), 50% (in progress), 75% (mostly done), 100% (fully achieved). Write one sentence of evidence for each score.

Step 3 — Gap identification
Based on the scores, which 1-2 Key Results have the biggest gap? What specifically caused the gap — time, difficulty, or wrong KR design?

Step 4 — Next-quarter OKRs
Write a new set of OKRs for the next 30 days of your PM learning journey. These should directly address the gaps identified in Step 3 and build on the competencies where you are already On Track.
Format: 1 Objective + 3 Key Results.

Feedback format:
WHAT WORKED IN YOUR OKR DESIGN: [were the KRs measurable? were they leading indicators or lagging?]
WHAT TO CHANGE: [OKR design issues — activity KRs disguised as outcome KRs, KRs that were too ambitious for 21 days]
COACHING NOTE: [one OKR design habit to carry into the next quarter]

Begin with Step 1 — ask me to paste my Day 15 OKRs.

bonusTasks.title: Search for APM hiring timelines and processes at Indian companies
bonusTasks.keyword: APM associate product manager hiring process timeline Flipkart Razorpay Swiggy 2024 2025 India
bonusTasks.why: Knowing the actual APM hiring timeline and process at companies you wants to target helps him translate the 21-day programme into a concrete application strategy — when to apply, what each round tests, and how to sequence his preparation.

assessmentTask.taskDescription: Three-part final assessment: Part 1 — full end-to-end PM case on a fictional B2B SaaS company (30 min). Part 2 — self-score across all 12 Reforge competencies with evidence from 21 days (15 min). Part 3 — personal next-30-days improvement plan (15 min).
assessmentTask.rawMaterial:
PART 1 — Full PM Case: SaaSCraft Churn Crisis (30 minutes)

SaaSCraft is a fictional Indian B2B project management SaaS serving SMEs (small teams of 5-50 people in Tier 1 and Tier 2 cities). Monthly subscription: ₹999/team. Current metrics:
- Monthly churn: increased from 3.2% to 3.8% QoQ (18% increase in churn rate)
- Trial-to-paid conversion: dropped from 41% to 34%
- Time to first value (completing first project in-app): avg 8 days (was 5 days last quarter)
- Feature adoption: Task management 89%, Reporting 12%, Integrations 8%

Complaint log (last 90 days, 847 total complaints):
- 42%: 'too complex for our team to learn'
- 28%: 'mobile app is unusable, we need mobile'
- 19%: 'integrations keep breaking (Google Workspace, Slack)'
- 11%: 'pricing too high vs alternatives like Trello/Notion'

10 proposed fixes from the engineering team:
1. Redesign onboarding with guided setup wizard
2. Rebuild mobile app (iOS + Android) from scratch
3. Add WhatsApp notifications for task updates
4. Fix and rebuild Google Workspace + Slack integrations
5. Add in-app tutorials for the Reporting feature
6. Add regional language support (Hindi, Tamil, Telugu)
7. Introduce flexible pricing tiers (Basic / Pro / Enterprise)
8. Build a 'simplified mode' toggle for first-time users
9. Launch a dedicated customer success programme
10. Add bulk import from Excel and Google Sheets

Your team: 3 engineers for the next 8 weeks.

Produce ALL of the following:
1. Problem statement (MECE, one sentence)
2. Top hypothesis for why churn increased (with evidence from the data)
3. RICE scores for the 10 proposed fixes — pick your top 3 with justification
4. One-paragraph mini PRD for your top-priority fix (problem, user story, success metric, one edge case)
5. A 5-line leadership update: what the data shows, what you are doing, what you need

PART 2 — Self-score across all 12 Reforge competencies (15 minutes)

For each of the 12 competencies, give yourself a score:
- Needs Focus: I studied this but cannot do it independently yet
- On Track: I can apply this with some support
- Outperform: I can apply this independently and explain my reasoning

Rate each with one sentence of evidence from your 21 days:
1. Feature Specification
2. Product Delivery
3. Product Quality
4. Fluency with Data
5. Voice of Customer
6. UX Design Sense
7. Business Outcome Ownership
8. Product Vision + Roadmapping
9. Strategic Impact
10. Stakeholder Management
11. Team Leadership
12. Managing Up

PART 3 — Personal next-30-days plan (15 minutes)

Based on your self-score in Part 2:
1. Name the 3 competencies with the biggest gap
2. For each, write one specific action you will take in the next 30 days to improve it — not 'read more about it' but a specific activity, case, or real-world practice
3. Name one competency where you are already On Track or Outperform, and write one way you will deepen it further
assessmentTask.outputFormat: Part 1: Five labelled deliverables (Problem Statement / Top Hypothesis / RICE table with top 3 chosen / Mini PRD paragraph / Leadership update). Part 2: 12 labelled competency ratings each with one evidence sentence. Part 3: Three gap competencies each with a specific 30-day action + one strength with deepening plan. Total: aim for 900-1100 words.
assessmentTask.claudePrompt:
You are a senior PM and programme mentor reviewing my final Day 21 assessment — the culmination of a 21-day APM foundations programme based on the Reforge 12 PM Competency Model.

I completed three parts:
- Part 1: Full PM case on SaaSCraft (fictional Indian B2B SaaS with 18% QoQ churn increase) — problem statement, hypothesis, RICE scores, mini PRD, leadership update
- Part 2: Self-score across all 12 Reforge competencies with evidence from 21 days
- Part 3: Personal next-30-days improvement plan

I studied across 21 days:
- Week 1: PM role, MECE, JTBD/Personas, Problem Framing, RICE, CIRCLES, integration
- Week 2: North Star + AARRR + SQL, RCA, A/B testing, Cohort analysis, PRDs + Figma, Roadmapping + Stakeholder comms, integration
- Week 3: Strategy + Market sizing + OKRs, Indian product landscape, Bharat vs India, Case practice (product design + estimation + API), Case practice (metrics + strategy), Behavioral cases + STAR stories, Final integration

Here is my work:
[Paste your work all three parts here]

Review my work and give feedback in EXACTLY this format — do not change these headers:

PART 1 FEEDBACK:
WHAT I GOT RIGHT:
- [specific bullets — was the problem statement MECE and specific? Did the top hypothesis cite the complaint data (42% 'too complex') rather than guessing? Were RICE scores internally consistent with the 3-engineer, 8-week constraint? Did the mini PRD include a measurable success metric? Was the leadership update in outcomes language, not activity language?]

WHAT NEEDS CORRECTION:
- [specific bullets — flag if: problem statement is vague or not MECE; RICE scores do not reflect the team size constraint (bulk import from Excel is Effort 1 week but onboarding redesign is Effort 6 weeks — if you scored them the same effort, flag this); the mini PRD does not name a specific user and uses generic language; the leadership update asks for 'support' instead of a specific resource or decision]

BLIND SPOTS:
- [things you did not consider — e.g., the onboarding complexity problem (42% of complaints) and the mobile problem (28%) are both symptoms of the same root cause: SaaSCraft was designed desktop-first and the user base has shifted to mobile-first SMEs; fixing onboarding without fixing mobile is treating the symptom; the complaint data combined with the 'time to first value' increase from 5 to 8 days strongly suggests the onboarding redesign should be the top priority — a guided wizard that works on mobile AND desktop addresses both the complexity and mobile problems in one build]

PART 2 FEEDBACK:
SELF-ASSESSMENT CALIBRATION:
- [2-3 bullets on whether your self-scores are realistic — are there competencies he over-rated (e.g., claiming Outperform on Strategic Impact after one week of strategy content) or under-rated (e.g., Needs Focus on Fluency with Data when he completed SQL readings, cohort analysis, RCA trees, and metric cases)?]

PART 3 FEEDBACK:
IMPROVEMENT PLAN QUALITY:
- [are the 30-day actions specific and executable, or are they vague intentions? e.g., 'practice more case interviews' is vague; 'do one timed metric drop case per week and get feedback from Claude using the Day 19 assessment prompt' is specific]

PROGRAMME SYNTHESIS:
- [2-3 sentences: which 2 competencies showed the clearest growth across 21 days based on your self-assessment and the quality of his Part 1 work? Which 1-2 need the most focused work in the next phase?]

FINAL SCORE: [X/10]
REFORGE COMPETENCY RATINGS (override your self-assessment where you disagree):
- Feature Specification: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Product Delivery: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Product Quality: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Fluency with Data: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Voice of Customer: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- UX Design Sense: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Business Outcome Ownership: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Product Vision + Roadmapping: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Strategic Impact: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Stakeholder Management: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Team Leadership: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
- Managing Up: [Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform]
